children.name: Rhea
children.description: Large flightless birds native to South America, superficially resembling ostriches but smaller and adapted to temperate grasslands and open woodlands. Both species are gregarious and polygynous.

children.name: Rhea americana
children.description: The greater rhea is a species of flightless bird native to eastern South America. Other names for the greater rhea include the grey, common, or American rhea; ema (Portuguese); or ñandú. One of two species in the genus Rhea, in the family Rheidae, it inhabits a variety of open areas, such as grasslands, savanna or grassy wetlands.

children.name: Rhea pennata
children.description: Darwin's rhea or the lesser rhea is a large flightless bird, the smaller of the two extant species of rheas. It is found in the Altiplano and Patagonia in South America.
